matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators lack standardized PD materials, coaching rubrics, and lesson frameworks — all of which can be generated at scale from curriculum standards and observed competency gaps. AI can produce site-specific coaching feedback scripts, observation forms, and structured lesson plan templates that currently don't exist or exist inconsistently across campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted professional development content engine that generates tailored coaching guides, competency-mapped lesson plan templates, and post-observation feedback letters for each teacher based on their subject and assessed skill gaps.

matches.match_rationale: Peer observation notes, training session transcripts, and instructional coaching reports accumulate but are rarely synthesized into actionable patterns. Summarization can distill observation records and PD session notes into per-teacher competency profiles and site-level trend summaries that instructional leaders can act on.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Automated synthesis of coaching logs, observation notes, and PD attendance records into quarterly teacher competency snapshots, surfacing patterns like repeated pedagogical weaknesses or subjects with persistent knowledge gaps across a site.

matches.match_rationale: Observation notes, self-assessments, and lesson reflections submitted by teachers contain signals about pedagogical competency and subject mastery that are currently unanalyzed. Text classification can tag these inputs against a competency framework, identifying which domains (e.g., differentiation, content knowledge, formative assessment) are consistently weak across teachers or sites.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A competency tagging system that classifies teacher observation write-ups and self-reflection entries against a standardized pedagogical framework, producing a heat map of skill gaps by teacher, subject, and campus.

matches.match_rationale: Teacher turnover at specific campuses creates recurring competency gaps; predicting which teachers are at risk of attrition allows operators to intervene with targeted support before the gap opens. Features like PD engagement, performance scores, campus tenure, and subject assignment can feed a propensity model for attrition likelihood.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A teacher retention risk model that scores each staff member's probability of leaving within 12 months, enabling targeted coaching investment and succession planning before competency gaps materialize.

matches.match_rationale: Teachers at under-resourced campuses often lack access to high-quality pedagogical resources and subject-matter guidance on demand. A RAG system grounded in curriculum frameworks, pedagogy research, and the operator's own lesson libraries allows teachers to ask specific instructional questions and receive grounded, contextually appropriate answers without waiting for a coaching session.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A teacher-facing instructional assistant that answers questions like 'How do I scaffold fractions for struggling Year 4 students?' by retrieving from an internal library of lesson plans, curriculum documents, and vetted pedagogy resources — reducing dependence on senior staff availability.

matches.match_rationale: A persistent conversational coaching agent can provide ongoing, low-cost instructional coaching between formal observation cycles — addressing the 'one-off, not reinforced' training failure pattern. Teachers can engage it for lesson planning support, reflective practice prompts, and subject knowledge clarification across multiple sessions.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI instructional coach that engages teachers in structured reflective dialogues after lessons, guides them through lesson planning using the operator's pedagogical framework, and tracks competency development over time without requiring a human coach to be present.

matches.match_rationale: Scheduling peer observations, routing coaching assignments, and distributing PD materials across 500+ staff at multiple sites involves coordination overhead that prevents the system from functioning. An automation agent can manage observation scheduling, send pre/post observation prompts, log outcomes, and escalate unresolved competency flags to instructional leaders.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A PD coordination agent that automatically schedules peer observation pairs, sends structured reflection prompts before and after observations, and routes flagged competency gaps to the appropriate instructional coach or subject lead — replacing the manual coordination that currently causes the system to collapse.

matches.match_rationale: Distributing limited instructional coaches and PD facilitators across multiple campuses to address the highest-priority competency gaps is a constrained assignment problem. AI can optimize coach deployment schedules based on competency gap severity, teacher caseloads, and travel constraints across sites.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A coaching allocation optimizer that assigns available instructional coaches to teachers and campuses based on assessed gap severity, coach subject expertise, and site visit schedules — maximizing coverage of the most critical competency deficits within existing staffing budgets.
